description: Remove unreachable response.ok check after fetchFromPage in instagram-api.ts.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/instagram && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove dead response.ok check after fetchFromPage in instagram-api.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/instagram/src/instagram-api.ts, the api() function has an `if (!response.ok) return classifyError(response, endpoint)` check (around line 96) after a fetchFromPage call. Since fetchFromPage auto-throws a ToolError on any non-ok HTTP response and never returns a non-ok Response, this check and the classifyError call are unreachable dead code. Remove the dead check.
acceptanceCriteria: The `if (!response.ok)` block and classifyError call on the non-ok branch are removed from the fetchFromPage path in instagram-api.ts
classifyError() is only retained if it is used elsewhere in the file
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/instagram/src/instagram-api.ts (remove dead if (!response.ok) after fetchFromPage, around line 96)

Context:
The fetchFromPage call is followed by:
  if (!response.ok) {
    return classifyError(response, endpoint); // UNREACHABLE
  }
  if (response.status === 204) return {} as T;
  ...

Since fetchFromPage throws on non-ok, simply delete the `if (!response.ok) { ... }` block. The 204 check and JSON parsing that follow remain.

If classifyError() is not used anywhere else in the file after this removal, also check if it can be deleted.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the 204 handling that follows
- Do NOT change any other error handling logic in the file
